posts.caption:
Backstab adds weapon dice based on thief level when the target is unaware.

GMs: Make the thief declare their Backstab before rolling; they add one weapon die per two levels when unnoticed.

Daily tabletop RPG rulings. Follow @bizzalgames70 for one a day. Full videos on YouTube: @Bizzal_Games

#ttrpg #rpg #shorts #reels #tabletoprpg #osrrpg #dungeonmaster #shadowdark #osr #oldschoolrpg

Oath of Glory allows paladins to shift roles between setup and closer effectively.

DMs: Have your Oath of Glory paladin use Peerless Athlete for crucial Strength checks, not trivial tasks, to control the encounter's pace.

Daily tabletop RPG rulings. Follow @bizzalgames70 for one a day. Full videos on YouTube: @Bizzal_Games

#ttrpg #rpg #shorts #reels #dnd5e #dungeonsanddragons #dungeonmaster #dmtips #ttrpgcommunity #tabletopgames

Two-Weapon Fighting reduces attack dice size based on Agility.

Judges: If their Agility is below 16, remind them they're just flailing with those smaller dice.

Daily tabletop RPG rulings. Follow @bizzalgames70 for one a day. Full videos on YouTube: @Bizzal_Games

#ttrpg #rpg #shorts #reels #oldschoolrenaissance #tabletoprpg #ttrpgcommunity #dcc #dungeoncrawlclassics #osr

Backstab grants extra weapon dice when hitting an unaware target.

GMs: Have the thief declare Backstab before rolling to ensure they get the extra damage dice without argument.

Daily tabletop RPG rulings. Follow @bizzalgames70 for one a day. Full videos on YouTube: @Bizzal_Games

#ttrpg #rpg #shorts #reels #tabletoprpg #osrrpg #dungeonmaster #shadowdark #osr #oldschoolrpg

Oath of Glory paladins pivot roles between setup and closer based on party needs.

DMs: Remind your Oath of Glory paladin to use Channel Divinity tactically, alternating between ally support and enemy takedown as needed.

Daily tabletop RPG rulings. Follow @bizzalgames70 for one a day. Full videos on YouTube: @Bizzal_Games

#ttrpg #rpg #shorts #reels #dnd5e #dungeonsanddragons #dungeonmaster #dmtips #ttrpgcommunity #tabletopgames

Word of Radiance

Word of Radiance is the spell people joke about - right before it saves a turn cycle.

Word of Radiance is a cantrip that deals 1d6 radiant damage and requires a Constitution saving throw. It targets all chosen creatures within a 5-foot sphere, not just one. Casting it against high-Constitution enemies often results in failed damage rolls. Better results occur when used against multiple low-Constitution targets clustered around the caster, increasing the likelihood of successful damage application.

DMs: When you're surrounded by weaklings, unleash Word of Radiance to exploit

Bugbear Stalker

Low-threat on paper, high-chaos at the table: Bugbear Stalker.

AC 15 and HP 65 mean it can take a few hits while executing its plan. With a Stealth bonus of +6, it often surprises parties, making the first move count. Abduct allows it to reposition enemies easily, disrupting formations. Ignoring this mobility can lead to disastrous positioning errors. Focus fire on the Bugbear Stalker to prevent it from dictating the battle's flow.

Players: laugh first, then respect turn economy before this joke encounter snowballs.

#dnd #dnd5e #ttrpg #rpg #shorts #reels

Wizard

Wizard spells scale dramatically at level 5 with access to 3rd-level spells.

A Wizard's power spike occurs at level 5 when 3rd-level spells become available. Many players underestimate the impact of spells like Fireball, which deals 8d6 damage in a 20-foot radius. Mismanagement occurs when players rely on low-level spells or cantrips instead of utilizing these more potent options. Optimal play involves planning spell slots and positioning to maximize area effects and manage resources effectively.

DMs: Remind your Wizards that at level 5, 8d6 Fireball beats cantrips, so they should st

The Order of Combat

The Order of Combat dictates that initiative determines turn sequence, not speed or dexterity.

Initiative, not dexterity modifiers or movement speed, decides who acts first in combat. Conflating dexterity with initiative leads to confusion when slower characters act before faster ones. This misunderstanding can disrupt strategy and timing. Planning actions based on actual turn order, determined by initiative rolls, prevents misplaced assumptions about character speed.

DMs: Remind your players that page 13 clearly states initiative order dictates combat turns, not dexteri

Ancient Red Dragon

Ancient Red Dragon is perfect for a playful encounter that can still punish bad habits.

With an Armor Class of 22 and 507 hit points, the Ancient Red Dragon presents a formidable challenge. Its commanding presence can disrupt party coordination, while its fiery breath weapon deals 26d6 fire damage in a 90-foot cone. Underestimating the dragon's 80-foot fly speed allows it to dictate engagement range, making it crucial to use terrain to limit its movement options. Forcing the dragon into predictable attack patterns can mitigate its battlefield control.

DMs: Use a cliff or

Ancient Green Dragon

Use Ancient Green Dragon as 'comic relief' that quietly pressures the party's decisions.

With an Armor Class of 21 and 402 hit points, the Ancient Green Dragon is a formidable opponent, but its true strength lies in its ability to control the battlefield. Its Mind Invasion ability, combined with a Multiattack, can force opponents into costly decisions. Many players underestimate the dragon's ability to manipulate terrain and positioning, often focusing too much on direct attacks. Optimal play requires leveraging terrain features to mitigate the dragon's control, forcing

Ancient Copper Dragon

Light tone, real consequences: build the scene around Ancient Copper Dragon and table overconfidence.

With an armor class of 21 and 367 hit points, the Ancient Copper Dragon is a formidable opponent. Its Acid Breath attack can impose significant pressure, dealing damage that is often underestimated due to the dragon's immunity to acid. Misjudging the dragon's Giggling Magic can lead to overlooking its ability to use spells creatively in combat. Instead of focusing solely on direct attacks, leveraging the terrain to limit the dragon's movement can shift the battle's mome

Spidersilk's duration is Focus, not a set time limit.

GMs: Demand players state their intent and target before casting Spidersilk, as its Focus duration demands strategic precision.

Daily tabletop RPG rulings. Follow @bizzalgames70 for one a day. Full videos on YouTube: @Bizzal_Games

Vulture is the classic 'easy fight' that gets weird fast if players autopilot.

DMs: run it with a wink, then punish lazy positioning once so the lesson lands.

Daily tabletop RPG rulings. Follow @bizzalgames70 for one a day. Full videos on YouTube: @Bizzal_Games

Scrolls of the Forbidden Summoning compel a Will DC 13 check or force the caster to complete the ritual.

Judges: Only use the Scrolls of the Forbidden Summoning if the party has a solid plan for an unbound, hostile demon.

Daily tabletop RPG rulings. Follow @bizzalgames70 for one a day. Full videos on YouTube: @Bizzal_Games

Word of Radiance

Word of Radiance is the spell people joke about - right before it saves a turn cycle.

Word of Radiance is a cantrip that deals 1d6 radiant damage and requires a Constitution saving throw. It targets all chosen creatures within a 5-foot sphere, not just one. Casting it against high-Constitution enemies often results in failed damage rolls. Better results occur when used against multiple low-Constitution targets clustered around the caster, increasing the likelihood of successful damage application.

DMs: When you're surrounded by weaklings, unleash Word of Radiance to exploit their low Constitution and clear the rabble.

#dnd #dnd5e #ttrpg #rpg #shorts #reels

Bugbear Stalker

Low-threat on paper, high-chaos at the table: Bugbear Stalker.

AC 15 and HP 65 mean it can take a few hits while executing its plan. With a Stealth bonus of +6, it often surprises parties, making the first move count. Abduct allows it to reposition enemies easily, disrupting formations. Ignoring this mobility can lead to disastrous positioning errors. Focus fire on the Bugbear Stalker to prevent it from dictating the battle's flow.

Players: laugh first, then respect turn economy before this joke encounter snowballs.

#dnd #dnd5e #ttrpg #rpg #shorts #reels

Wizard

Wizard spells scale dramatically at level 5 with access to 3rd-level spells.

A Wizard's power spike occurs at level 5 when 3rd-level spells become available. Many players underestimate the impact of spells like Fireball, which deals 8d6 damage in a 20-foot radius. Mismanagement occurs when players rely on low-level spells or cantrips instead of utilizing these more potent options. Optimal play involves planning spell slots and positioning to maximize area effects and manage resources effectively.

DMs: Remind your Wizards that at level 5, 8d6 Fireball beats cantrips, so they should stop hoarding spell slots.

#dnd #dnd5e #ttrpg #rpg #shorts #reels

The Order of Combat

The Order of Combat dictates that initiative determines turn sequence, not speed or dexterity.

Initiative, not dexterity modifiers or movement speed, decides who acts first in combat. Conflating dexterity with initiative leads to confusion when slower characters act before faster ones. This misunderstanding can disrupt strategy and timing. Planning actions based on actual turn order, determined by initiative rolls, prevents misplaced assumptions about character speed.

DMs: Remind your players that page 13 clearly states initiative order dictates combat turns, not dexterity or speed.

#dnd #dnd5e #ttrpg #rpg #shorts #reels

Investiture of Flame grants immunity to fire damage and resistance to cold damage for up to 10 minutes.

DMs: Use Investiture of Flame when fire immunity will nullify an enemy's main attack, not just for the 1d10 damage.

Ran it differently at your table? Comments are open. @bizzalgames70

#ttrpg #rpg #reels #dungeonsanddragons #dungeonmaster #dmtips #ttrpgcommunity #tabletopgames #dndmemes

Light tone, real consequences: build the scene around Cacophony Golem and table overconfidence.

Judges: Remind them that spells have a 50% failure rate and the golem's 0' movement makes it a sitting duck for arrows.

Daily tabletop RPG rulings. Follow @bizzalgames70 for one a day.

Yes, Assassin looks harmless - until the table underestimates it and gives it free turns.

DMs: run it with a wink, then punish lazy positioning once so the lesson lands.

One ruling a day, no fluff. Follow @bizzalgames70.

#ttrpg #rpg #reels #dungeoncrawl #indierpg #ttrpgcommunity #tabletoprpg #osrrpg #dungeonmaster

Unseen Attackers and Targets impose Disadvantage on attack rolls against unseen targets.

DMs: Enforce Disadvantage on attacks against unseen targets until visibility is restored; hearing alone doesn't cut it.

Ran it differently at your table? Comments are open. @bizzalgames70

#ttrpg #rpg #reels #dnd #dnd5e #dungeonsanddragons #dungeonmaster #dmtips #ttrpgcommunity

Halflings have the best Luck economy in the game.

Judges: Remind your Halfling to use their Luck die for stealth checks; it's the consistent edge they keep forgetting.

Daily tabletop RPG rulings. Follow @bizzalgames70 for one a day.

#ttrpg #rpg #reels #ttrpgcommunity #dcc #dungeoncrawlclassics #osr #goodmangames #dungeonmaster

Spell Casting Check consumes the slot on failure.

GMs: Ensure players calculate DC as 10 plus spell tier before rolling to prevent unnecessary spell slot loss.

One ruling a day, no fluff. Follow @bizzalgames70.

#ttrpg #rpg #reels #dungeoncrawl #indierpg #ttrpgcommunity #tabletoprpg #osrrpg #dungeonmaster

Barrier Tattoo only works without armor.

DMs: Remind players that Barrier Tattoo's AC bonus is void if they wear armor; plan attunement logistics before combat.

Bookmark it before your players find the loophole. @bizzalgames70

#ttrpg #rpg #reels #dnd5e #dungeonsanddragons #dungeonmaster #dmtips #ttrpgcommunity #tabletopgames

Two-Weapon Fighting uses smaller dice along the dice chain with both weapons.

Judges: Remind them that without 13+ Agility, their two-weapon dreams are just a dice chain downgrade.

One ruling a day, no fluff. Follow @bizzalgames70.

Judges: Remind them that spells have a 50% failure rate and the golem's 0' movement makes it a sitting duck for arrows.

Spidersilk's duration is Focus, not a set time limit.

GMs: Demand players state their intent and target before casting Spidersilk, as its Focus duration demands strategic precision.

Judges: Only use the Scrolls of the Forbidden Summoning if the party has a solid plan for an unbound, hostile demon.

Backstab adds weapon dice based on thief level when the target is unaware.

GMs: Make the thief declare their Backstab before rolling; they add one weapon die per two levels when unnoticed.
